beginner.label: Beginner
beginner.description: Everyday survival English. Short sentences, present/past tense, common vocabulary.

scenarios.title: Introducing yourself
scenarios.opener: Play a friendly new neighbor. Greet the user and ask their name and where they're from, in very simple English.

scenarios.title: Ordering at a cafe
scenarios.opener: Play a cafe barista. Greet the customer and ask what they'd like to order, using simple everyday English.

scenarios.title: Asking for directions
scenarios.opener: Play a stranger on the street. The user will ask you for directions to a train station. Respond helpfully in simple English.

intermediate.label: Intermediate
intermediate.description: Natural conversational flow, connected speech, common idioms, opinions and small talk.

scenarios.title: Small talk with a coworker
scenarios.opener: Play a coworker chatting near the coffee machine. Make natural small talk about the weekend and ask a follow-up question.

scenarios.title: Booking a doctor's appointment by phone
scenarios.opener: Play a clinic receptionist on the phone. Ask the caller what the problem is and offer appointment times.

scenarios.title: Discussing a movie or show
scenarios.opener: Play a friend who just watched a popular movie. Share a brief opinion and ask the user what they thought about a similar topic.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.description: Nuance, persuasion, debate, storytelling, and idiomatic, native-like phrasing.

scenarios.title: Friendly debate
scenarios.opener: Play a friend who mildly disagrees with the user on whether remote work is better than office work. Open with a light, respectful challenge.

scenarios.title: Job interview - tough questions
scenarios.opener: Play a hiring manager. Ask a challenging behavioral interview question such as describing a time the candidate handled conflict.

scenarios.title: Telling a story with follow-ups
scenarios.opener: Ask the user to tell you about a memorable trip, then plan to ask engaged follow-up questions that push for more descriptive, native-like detail.

business.label: Business & Accounting English
business.description: Meetings, presenting numbers, client calls, negotiation, and finance vocabulary.

scenarios.title: Leading a status meeting
scenarios.opener: Play a colleague joining the user's project status meeting. Ask them to walk you through this quarter's progress.

scenarios.title: Presenting quarterly numbers
scenarios.opener: Play a manager. Ask the user to summarize the company's revenue and expenses for the last quarter, and probe on any variance.

scenarios.title: Client call about an invoice
scenarios.opener: Play a client who is asking about a discrepancy on an invoice. Raise the concern politely and ask the user to explain the charges.

scenarios.title: Negotiating a budget
scenarios.opener: Play a department head pushing back on the user's proposed budget increase. Open by questioning one specific line item.
